# Democraft

Democraft turns real product workflows into rendered Remotion videos.

Core packages:

- `@democraft/core`: TypeScript authoring API.
- `@democraft/compiler`: compiles demos into JSON-compatible IR.
- `@democraft/playwright`: executes browser workflows and records manifests.
- `@democraft/timeline`: resolves render timelines from IR and manifests.
- `@democraft/remotion`: renders videos from timelines.
- `@democraft/cli`: agent-friendly inspection, validation, capture, preview, and render commands.

Authoring pattern:

```ts
import { byTestId, defineDemo, defineTargets } from "@democraft/core";

const targets = defineTargets({
  dashboard: byTestId("dashboard"),
});

export default defineDemo({
  id: "create-project",
  title: "Create Project",
  source: { baseUrl: "http://localhost:3000" },
  targets,
  async run({ demo }) {
    await demo.scene("intro", async (scene) => {
      await scene.goto("/dashboard");
      await scene.expectVisible("dashboard");
    });
  },
});
```

Agent workflow:

```bash
democraft targets ./demos/create-project/demo.ts --json
democraft inspect ./demos/create-project/demo.ts --json
democraft validate ./demos/create-project/demo.ts --static --json
democraft capture ./demos/create-project/demo.ts --output-dir .democraft/runs/create-project
democraft timeline ./demos/create-project/demo.ts --manifest .democraft/runs/create-project/manifest.json --output-file .democraft/timelines/create-project.landscape.json
democraft preview --manifest .democraft/runs/create-project/manifest.json --timeline .democraft/timelines/create-project.landscape.json --output-file .democraft/previews/create-project.html
democraft render --manifest .democraft/runs/create-project/manifest.json --timeline .democraft/timelines/create-project.landscape.json --output-file .democraft/renders/create-project.mp4
```

Remocn integration:

Remocn components are copied into the user's project with shadcn, for example:

```bash
pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @remocn/soft-blur-in
```

The renderer should map semantic renderer IDs to copied Remocn components through a visual registry. Demo source should reference portable IDs such as `renderer: "remocn.soft-blur-in"` instead of importing Remocn components directly.

Guardrails:

- Keep target IDs stable.
- Prefer literal locators from `defineTargets()`.
- Run `validate --static --json` before capture.
- Use structured diagnostics to repair target IDs, locators, durations, and renderer IDs.
